mission.title: Install and Configure SlimToolkit on Kubernetes
mission.description: SlimToolkit (formerly DockerSlim) optimizes container images by analyzing application behavior and removing unnecessary files, reducing image size by up to 30x while automatically generating security profiles (Seccomp, AppArmor).

steps.title: Install SlimToolkit CLI
steps.description:
Install the Slim CLI using the official install script or by downloading the binary directly.
```bash
# Option 1: Install script
curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/slimtoolkit/slim/master/scripts/install-slim.sh | sudo -E bash -

# Option 2: Homebrew (macOS/Linux)
brew install slim

# Verify installation
slim version
```

steps.title: Build an optimized container image
steps.description:
Use the slim build command to analyze and optimize a container image.
```bash
slim build --target your-image:latest --tag your-image:slim
```

steps.title: Inspect an image with xray
steps.description:
Use the xray command to inspect an image and understand its contents before optimizing.
```bash
slim xray your-image:latest
```

steps.title: Deploy optimized images to Kubernetes
steps.description:
Push the optimized image to your registry and update your Kubernetes deployment.
```bash
docker push your-registry/your-image:slim
kubectl set image deployment/your-app container=your-registry/your-image:slim
```

steps.title: Verify the optimized deployment
steps.description:
Check that pods are running successfully with the optimized image.
```bash
kubectl get pods
kubectl rollout status deployment/your-app
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the Slim CLI available for optimizing container images. Optimized images are smaller, more secure, and can be deployed to Kubernetes like any other container image.

uninstall.title: Remove the Slim CLI
uninstall.description: Delete the slim binary or uninstall via Homebrew: brew uninstall slim.

upgrade.title: Update the Slim CLI
upgrade.description: Re-run the install script or use brew upgrade slim to get the latest version.

troubleshooting.title: Optimized image missing runtime files
troubleshooting.description:
Use the --include-path flag to explicitly include files the dynamic analysis may have missed.
```bash
slim build --target your-image:latest --include-path /app/templates --include-path /app/static
```

troubleshooting.title: HTTP probing fails
troubleshooting.description:
If the application does not serve HTTP, disable HTTP probing.
```bash
slim build --target your-image:latest --http-probe=false
```

troubleshooting.title: Build fails with Docker permission error
troubleshooting.description: Ensure Docker is running and the current user has access to the Docker socket.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have Docker installed for image optimization and kubectl for deploying optimized images to Kubernetes.
